steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject is "Paramaribo" (singular). The prepositional phrase "with a population of roughly 250,000 people" modifies it but doesn't change its number.

steps.title: Match the verb to the subject
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: a singular subject takes a singular verb; intervening prepositional phrases do not affect agreement.
  • Choice A — "is": singular simple present; correctly agrees with singular subject "Paramaribo." ✓
  • Choice B — "are": plural simple present; does not agree with singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — "are being": plural progressive; does not agree with singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — "have been": plural present perfect; does not agree with singular subject. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
